title: Project Obsidian
subtitle: Iceland and the Faroe Islands on the Norwegian Star

team.note: Black volcanic glass, the real kind, made exactly the way Kilian makes it in Minecraft: lava meets water. Iceland is the biggest obsidian farm on Earth. Grandma and Grandpa are sailing too, on their own reservation.

bring: Waterproof hiking boots, broken in. Non-negotiable for Vigur and the sea cliffs
Base layer: wool or synthetic, no cotton
Mid layer: heavy fleece or wool
Outer shell: waterproof AND windproof. Umbrellas are useless here
Warm hat and gloves even in August. It is 50 to 55F with hard wind
Passports for everyone, plus the cruise boarding passes
Seasick meds for the Aug 15 crossing of the Iceland-Faroe Ridge
Swimsuits for the Forest Lagoon in Akureyri
Binoculars for the puffins and the whales
Portable battery and a plug adapter (Europlug Type F, 230V)

bookings.title: Flight out: Icelandair 852, ORD to KEF
bookings.note: Overnight crossing. Lands in Iceland the next morning.

bookings.title: Old Town Reykjavik
bookings.note: One night before the ship. Walkable to everything downtown.

bookings.title: Norwegian Star, 7 nights
bookings.note: Freestyle cruising. No fixed dining times, no formal nights.

bookings.title: Flight home: Icelandair 855, KEF to ORD
bookings.note: Lands O'Hare 9:25pm the same evening. Flying west you gain 5 hours back.

bookings.title: Blue Lagoon, last day
bookings.note: Book a mid-morning slot. It sells out and there are eleven hours to fill between walking off the ship and the flight.

bookings.title: Flybus: cruise terminal to Blue Lagoon to airport
bookings.note: About $29 to $35 a head instead of the ship's $99.50. Book online.

bookings.title: Online check-in with Norwegian
bookings.note: Opens Jul 19, 21 days out. Needs passport numbers, an emergency contact who is not sailing, and a card for the onboard account.

bookings.title: Vigur Island puffin tour
bookings.note: Last window of the season. The birds leave mid-August. Private operators want months of lead time, so this is already tight.

bookings.title: Do NOT buy the ship's airport transfer
bookings.note: $99.50 a head, and it drops you at Keflavik at 9am for a 7:45pm flight. Wrong plan and four times the price. Flybus via the Blue Lagoon instead.

lists.title: The whole point
lists.note: The reasons we flew across an ocean. Do not come home without these.

items.text: Find real obsidian. Actual volcanic glass, in the wild

items: Puffins on Vigur Island before they leave for the winter
Kirkjufell, the most photographed mountain in Iceland
Stand in the Faroe Islands, a place most people never get to

lists.title: If the weather cooperates
lists.note: North Atlantic weather decides these, not us.
items: Godafoss, the waterfall of the gods
Soak in the Forest Lagoon infinity pools above Akureyri
Spot the heart-shaped traffic lights in Akureyri

notes: Tipping is not expected in Iceland or the Faroe Islands. Service is already in the price at restaurants and in taxis.
The Faroes run one hour ahead of Iceland in summer. The ship stays on Iceland time. Do not trust your phone in Torshavn.
Iceland is card-first. You can go the whole trip without touching cash.
Grundarfjordur is a tender port. Get tender tickets the moment they release or you lose shore hours standing in line.
Isafjordur all-aboard at 2pm is hard. Do not go more than 30 minutes from the pier on your own.
closingNote: Salt in the air, obsidian in the backpack, everybody home safe.

places.name: Chicago O'Hare
places.desc: International departures. Bag drop closes an hour before the flight.

places.name: Keflavik Airport
places.desc: 45 minutes from Reykjavik, 20 from the Blue Lagoon. The Flybus runs both legs.

places.name: Blue Lagoon
places.desc: Between the city and the airport, and it stores your luggage. The answer to the 11-hour gap on the last day. Book ahead, it sells out.

places.name: Old Town Reykjavik
places.desc: One night before the ship, right on the old town square.

places.name: Skarfabakki Pier 312
places.desc: The cruise pier. 10 to 12 minutes from downtown by taxi, about 4,000 ISK.

places.name: Grundarfjordur
places.desc: Tender port. The village is 500m from the landing.

places.name: Kirkjufell
places.desc: The arrowhead mountain, 3km from the pier. The waterfall is on the near side.

places.name: Vatnshellir Lava Cave
places.desc: 8,000-year-old lava tubes on Snaefellsnes. Ages 5 and up.

places.name: Akureyri, Oddeyrarbryggja
places.desc: Capital of the north. The ship docks right at the town.

places.name: Godafoss
places.desc: The waterfall of the gods, 45 minutes east of Akureyri.

places.name: Forest Lagoon
places.desc: Infinity pools in a birch forest above the fjord.

places.name: Isafjordur, Sundabakki
places.desc: Westfjords hub. Walk-off pier. Hard 2pm all-aboard.

places.name: Vigur Island
places.desc: 100,000 puffins, plus eiders and arctic terns. They start leaving mid-August.

places.name: Dynjandi Waterfall
places.desc: Tiered cliff waterfall. Getting there means mountain tunnels that can delay you.

places.name: Seydisfjordur
places.desc: The rainbow street and the blue church, at the head of a narrow eastern fjord.

places.name: Torshavn, Faroe Islands
places.desc: The smallest capital in the world. Local time runs one hour ahead of the ship.

places.name: Vestmanna Sea Cliffs
places.desc: Basalt grottos full of nesting seabirds. A 45 minute drive before the boat even starts.

places.name: Kirkjubour
places.desc: Medieval village walk, 3 to 4 hours. The red bus back to the pier is free.

places.name: Tinganes Old Town
places.desc: Red turf-roofed government houses on the harbour spit. Easy walk from the ship.

days.title: Fly out
notes: Kilian skips the last day of camp, so this day has room in it now. Keep the room.

schedule.title: Drive to O'Hare
schedule.note: Leaving early is the whole plan. Friday evening into Chicago is the part that used to break.

schedule.title: Park and shuttle to the terminal
schedule.note: Off-site lots add 20 to 30 minutes. Terminal 5 for Icelandair.

schedule.title: Bag drop, security, gate
schedule.note: International bag drop closes 60 minutes out.

schedule.title: Icelandair 852 departs
schedule.note: Published schedule is 7:25pm, but the calendar says 7:45pm. Check the confirmation. Overnight, 6h15, lands 6:40am.

days.title: Land in Reykjavik
notes: You lose a night to the flight. Go easy and stay up until a normal bedtime.

schedule.title: Transfer into Reykjavik
schedule.note: 45 minutes. Flybus or a pre-booked van.

schedule.title: Drop bags, walk the old town
schedule.note: Rooms will not be ready this early. Leave the bags and go.

schedule.title: Reykjavik at your own pace
schedule.note: Hallgrimskirkja, the harbour, Braud and Co. Nothing scheduled on purpose.

days.title: Board the Star
notes: All aboard is 5pm, not 7pm. The ship does not wait.

schedule.title: Check out, last morning in town
Taxi to Skarfabakki Pier 312
schedule.note: Ask for a minivan. A sedan will not hold a family plus suitcases.

schedule.title: Muster drill, explore the ship
schedule.note: Sign Kilian up for Splash Academy today, not later in the week.

schedule.title: All aboard, then sailaway
schedule.note: Ship departs 7pm. Watch Reykjavik go by from the top deck.

days.title: Grundarfjordur
notes: Tender port. Tickets first, everything else second.

schedule.title: Arrive, get tender tickets
schedule.note: Do this immediately. Late tickets cost you hours ashore.

schedule.title: Kirkjufell and the Snaefellsnes coast
schedule.note: 3km from the pier. The mountain-and-waterfall shot everyone knows.

schedule.title: Vatnshellir lava cave
schedule.note: Underground and cold. Bring the fleece you were not going to bring.

schedule.title: Last tender back
schedule.note: Ship leaves at 4pm. Do not cut this fine.

days.title: Akureyri
notes: The longest day in port and the best one for whales.

schedule.title: Dock at Oddeyrarbryggja
schedule.note: Walk off, straight into town.

schedule.title: Whale watching in Eyjafjordur
schedule.note: The classic boat is 3 hours and calmer. The RIB is 2 hours, faster, wetter, closer.

schedule.title: Godafoss
schedule.note: 45 minutes each way. Shuttle runs about $92.

schedule.title: Forest Lagoon
schedule.note: Infinity pools over the fjord. Icelandic pool rules: you shower first, properly.

days.title: Isafjordur and the puffins
notes: Seven hours only, and a hard 2pm all-aboard. The tightest day of the cruise.

schedule.title: Dock at Sundabakki
schedule.note: Walk-off pier, right at the town.

schedule.title: Vigur Island puffin tour
schedule.note: The one thing on this trip with an expiry date. The colony starts leaving mid-August.

schedule.title: Isafjordur old town
schedule.note: Stay close to the pier. Do not wander.

schedule.title: All aboard. Hard stop
schedule.note: Skip Dynjandi unless you are on a ship excursion. The tunnels delay people and the ship will leave without you.

days.title: Seydisfjordur
notes: The prettiest approach of the trip. Be on deck for it.

schedule.title: Sail in through the fjord
schedule.note: Narrow eastern fjord, mountains both sides. Worth an alarm.

schedule.title: The rainbow street and the blue church
schedule.note: Small, walkable, and the best photo in Iceland that does not need a bus.

schedule.title: Waterfall walk, or just the town

days.title: Torshavn, Faroe Islands
notes: Faroe local time is one hour ahead of the ship. Your phone will lie to you. Trust ship time.

schedule.title: Dock in Torshavn
schedule.note: Every time here is SHIP time. Local clocks read an hour later.

schedule.title: Vestmanna sea cliffs
schedule.note: 45 minute drive before the boat even starts. Big day, big payoff.

schedule.title: Tinganes old town
schedule.note: Red turf-roofed houses on the harbour. Easy walk from the ship.

schedule.title: All aboard
schedule.note: Ship time. Check your watch against the ship, not your phone.

days.title: At sea
notes: Crossing the Iceland-Faroe Ridge. Sea state here is genuinely variable.

schedule.title: Sea day. Do nothing
schedule.note: Splash Academy, the pool, the spa. Take the meds early if the swell builds.

days.title: Blue Lagoon, then home
days.closingNote: Salt in the air, obsidian in the backpack, everybody home safe.
notes: You walk off at 7am and the plane leaves at 7:45pm. That is eleven hours. Do not spend them in an airport.
The Blue Lagoon sits between Reykjavik and Keflavik and stores luggage. That is the whole answer to this day.

schedule.title: Dock at Skarfabakki, walk off
schedule.note: Walk-off. Carry your own bags and you leave on your own schedule, not the ship's.

schedule.title: Transfer toward the Blue Lagoon
schedule.note: Flybus runs the cruise terminal to Blue Lagoon to airport route. Do not buy the ship's transfer for this.

schedule.title: Store the luggage
schedule.note: Roughly 550 ISK a bag, about $4. Lockers inside for the small stuff.

schedule.title: The Blue Lagoon
schedule.note: Book ahead. It sells out, and turning up without a slot means you do not get in.

schedule.title: Blue Lagoon to Keflavik
schedule.note: About 20 minutes. The Flybus does this leg on a schedule.

schedule.title: Check in, eat, fly
schedule.note: Icelandair 855 departs 7:45pm Iceland time and lands O'Hare 9:25pm the SAME evening. You gain 5 hours going west.
